purpose: Eleventh AS-owned schematic-linked Universal Cell trace, covering recipient-side rejection of a non-init command-message pulled from upstream.

ports.label: north
ports.summary: First signal channel, empty during this recipient rejection trace.

ports.label: east
ports.summary: Second signal channel carrying the upstream standard-signal command-message token.

ports.label: west
ports.summary: Third signal channel, empty during this recipient rejection trace.

layers.summary: A standard-signal command-message token on a recipient fixed cell is rejected rather than executed.
layers.boundary: This layer states the recipient non-init rejection boundary only; it does not implement standard-signal or write-buffer command execution.

layers.summary: The recipient rejection node uses the same triangular GELC/RLEM port vocabulary as the other traces.
layers.boundary: This layer fixes schematic geometry and port orientation, not a complete multi-cell command-routing circuit.

layers.summary: The trace records every current AS Cell field before and after one recipient non-init command-message rejection.
layers.boundary: This layer is limited to the ADR-0054 rejection claim and adds no non-init command execution semantics.

layers.summary: Switchable circulator hardware and auxiliary command-routing support remain candidate interpretations.
layers.boundary: This layer keeps physical implementation and RALA-like auxiliary circuitry separate from the tested rejection transition.

routed_signal_flow: command[standard-signal] rejected as recipient non-init command-message
